content: We can frame an LLM's iterative Self-Refine process as the computational equivalent of the human reflective practice that the English language vocabulary 'reflexion' (reflection) describes: just as human reflexion relies on stopping to review past outputs against implicit personal or social standards to adjust future thinking, Self-Refine operationalizes this exact cognitive loop for autonomous agents. A two-layer hybrid reflexive agent framework can be built where high-level human-style reflexion (rooted in normative value alignment learned from human text corpora) guides low-level iterative Self-Refine output tuning, creating outputs that are both technically refined and normatively aligned with human expectations.
